fr.recommendations: Facilitating effective emergency responses to the property could be achieved by clearly marking the single access point and ensuring its accessibility and visibility at all times. Enhancing signage and lighting in this area may assist responders in quickly locating the entryway during urgent situations. Additionally, providing open and unobstructed pathways to and from the main building and the detached structure could support smooth navigation and movement, particularly if vehicles need to traverse the internal drive aisle. The presence of open green space offers potential for emergency responder staging; ensuring this area is easily accessible and free from excessive vegetation or obstacles could enhance staging and operational abilities. Planning for clear sightlines across the property may further aid visibility and effective coordination between the structures during emergency operations.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the structure by increasing the separation between the adjacent vegetation and the building could be beneficial. Reducing the fuel concentrations in proximity to the structure and along the vegetated perimeter may further support fire resistance. With open terrain and an existing fuel break, maintaining these features will help to limit fire spread and contribute to long-term resilience. Ensuring the upkeep of the long driveway by managing vegetation alongside it can improve access for firefighting resources and support suppression activities. Addressing the scattered trees by assessing their placement and ensuring adequate spacing can help minimize fuel continuity across the property. Engaging in regular vegetation management, including the creation of strategic fuel mosaics, will enhance preparedness and provide effective breaks in vegetative fuel continuity.
